{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/0000035749.html Hirakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/masano-kurumazuka-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/0000035749.html Hirakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/masano-kurumazuka-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient burial mounds that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hirakata, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun]][[en:Kinyakurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:禁野車塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11591162]] Wikidata name: Kinyakurumazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11591162]] Wikidata name: Kinyakurumazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | native_name =禁野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinyakurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.810833333333|N|135.65722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|48|39.23|N|135|39|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinyakurumazuka Kofun'''|禁野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Miyanosaka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 with the area under protection expanded in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172555 |title=禁野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Kinyakurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinyakurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a residential area between the rivers of the Hirakata hills extending to the northwestern foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]] on the bank of the Amano River near its confluence with the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[:en:Keihan Katano Line|Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards posterior File:Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kin-ya Kurumazuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/0000035749.html Hirakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/masano-kurumazuka-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is one of the few ancient Kofuns that remain on the south bank of the Yodo River basin, and since the Yodo River was one of the main transportation routes from [[Seto Inland Sea]] to the center of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], it is regarded to be of great significance to understanding the Kofun period history of the area. The tumulus has a length of 120 meters and is orientated to the west. The anterior portion is low and has a "drumstick" shape, whereas the posterior circular portion is high. There is no moat, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the lower south side of the posterior circular portion and both cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and figurative ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus has been extensively surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has yet been conducted, so details of its [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain. It is presumed to be a vertical pit-type stone chamber based on piece of stone slab at the top of the posterior circle. Based on the shape and construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period, or around the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Miyanosaka Station (Osaka)|Miyanosaka Station]] on the [[:en:Keihan Katano Line|Keihan Katano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 120 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 55 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 63 meter diameter, 2-tiers
